title: Sport Analytics as a Tool for Effective Decision-Making
abstract: The strategy of sports can be understood through the decisions by the athletes and coaches. Decision-makers have to be aware of the rational structure of the games; however, on the field they have to rely on bounded rationality or intuition. By describing rational strategies sports analytics can support better decision-making and contribute to uncovering the hidden biases committed by sports people. Our chapter is to identify connection points between decision-making theory and sports analytics through basketball practices.
